summary: In high-bleeding-risk patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention, bare-metal stents (BMS) are still justifiable because they permit reduced duration of dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT), thereby lowering hemorrhagic complications while maintaining adequate outcomes compared to drug-eluting stents (DES). This benefit is particularly significant in scenarios where prolonged anticoagulation is not feasible or tolerated.
